method_atom: The spectral analysis of random matrices whose entries are deterministic functions of randomly distributed points in a metric space. For the translation-invariant case one generically finds a phase transition in the spectrum between two qualitatively distinct regimes (extended collective modes versus a regime dominated by unstable/negative-eigenvalue directions) as a control parameter crosses a critical value. The eigenvalue distribution and the transition are computed with field-theoretic methods that exploit a hidden supersymmetry of the ensemble.
object_atom: The Hessian of the Hamiltonian of the particles of a fluid, whose eigenmodes are the vibrational excitations; here the extended-mode-to-unstable-direction transition is identified with the dynamical (mode-coupling) phase transition of glasses, and the low-temperature Boson peak observed in glasses is interpreted as a remnant of it.
brief_reason: What carries across is the spectral-transition machinery of distance-dependent random matrices and its supersymmetric field-theoretic solution; what is domain-specific is the fluid/glass Hessian, the glass dynamical transition, and the Boson peak.
